implReport.e2eSummary: Proven: full lifecycle trigger->wait->condition->action->exit across ticks; wait deferral via real sweep query; branch routing via §8 compiler against real profile_features; idempotent advance (10x Promise.all -> exactly one completion + one outbox via CAS); enrollment via segment_change_log entered with 'once' ON CONFLICT (exited->none); cross-workspace isolation; ALL sends via real dispatchOutbox (suppressed skipped, unverified refused, runner never calls SES); cross-phase journey.
implReport.notes: Scope §9B only. Reused (no re-implement): @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch, @cdp/service-dispatcher dispatchOutbox + {outbox_id} contract, broadcast runStatementsInWorkspaceTx (asserts workspace_id $1), @cdp/db testutil. No new migration (0005). KEY FINDING: CAS on updated_at required ::text comparison — Postgres timestamptz microsecond vs JS Date millisecond precision means 'updated_at=$n::timestamptz' silently never re-matches; sweep/load/claim now compare updated_at::text for exact full-precision match. Re-enrollment 'once' structural (UNIQUE+ON CONFLICT) + decideReenrollment. Loop guard MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK=50. Teardown FK order respected. campaign-journey guards applyMigrations behind to_regclass check.
implReport.testSummary: campaign-runner 65 tests (8 unit files + 7 integration on real PG + handler) all green. Full repo 35/35 turbo tasks both cold runs. No regression in phases 2-9. Integration RUNS on real PG localhost:5433; SES+SQS mocked at boundary.

invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code; the admin app uses the user JWT so RLS applies.

workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or a trusted server-set field. The frontend sends the active-workspace claim via its JWT; the API resolves scope from the authorizer, never from a body field.

Role enforcement (§3A): owner/marketer/accounting are workspace-scoped; system-admin is the only cross-tenant role and every cross-tenant access is audit-logged. The UI shows only what the role permits; the API enforces it regardless of the UI.

All sends (broadcasts §9A and campaign actions §9B) go through the SAME outbox -> Dispatcher pipeline (§9) and pass suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours; sending is gated on workspace active/verified.

Idempotency: broadcasts dedupe per (broadcast_id, profile_id); the campaign runner must tolerate retries without double-advancing (optimistic checks / updated_at); image uploads + metering rollups are idempotent.

LOCAL-only: AWS (SES/SNS/S3/SQS) are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock) or run on LocalStack; never hit real AWS. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433. The editor must emit MJML, never hand-rolled email HTML.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin. Reuse the prior harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations; runPlanInWorkspaceTx; SqlStatement {text,values}; @cdp/segments resolveAudience; @cdp/email; the dispatcher core).

Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys). No cross-file races. Frontend e2e (Playwright) must be deterministic (seeded data, stable selectors).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no features beyond the spec. Keep the cost model EXACTLY as §20 (direct usage + even split of fixed costs).
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (localhost:5433); mock AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack. No frontend.

criteria: An enrolled profile advances through trigger -> wait -> condition -> action -> exit.
A wait defers until next_run_at; a branch (condition) routes correctly using the §8 compiler against profile/features/membership.
The runner is idempotent (no double-advance on retry / concurrent sweep).
Campaign action sends pass through the Dispatcher guards (suppression/cap/quiet-hours); enrollment via segment entry works; re-enrollment policy is enforced. Everything workspace-scoped.
scope: A campaign DSL: campaigns.definition is a graph of nodes [trigger|wait|condition|action|exit] + edges. Define the node schema in this phase.
Enrollment: a trigger (segment entry via segment_change_log, an event, or manual) inserts a campaign_enrollments row at the start node (UNIQUE (campaign_id, profile_id)).

services/campaign-runner: scheduled sweep WHERE status=active AND next_run_at <= now(); process the current node: wait -> set next_run_at; condition -> evaluate via the §8 compiler, pick next node, process immediately; action(send) -> insert outbox row -> Dispatcher; exit -> completed.
Idempotent advance (tolerate retries without double-advancing — use updated_at / optimistic checks). Define and implement the re-enrollment policy.
phase.specSections: §9B (table-driven per-profile state machine: nodes trigger/wait/condition/action/exit; Campaign-runner sweep on next_run_at; enrollment via segment entry), §8 (reuse compiler for branch conditions), §9 (Dispatcher for action sends), §6 (campaigns, campaign_enrollments, segment_change_log)
phase.title: Campaign workflow engine

implUnits: services/campaign-runner/src/core.ts pure decisions + SqlStatement builders (reuse @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch + broadcast enqueue contract)
services/campaign-runner/src/enroll.ts enrollment orchestrator (parse change_log -> decideReenrollment -> insert in workspace tx)
services/campaign-runner/src/run.ts runEnrollment: claim CAS -> process node chain single tick to wait/exit boundary -> enqueue sends -> advance; workspace_id from row
services/campaign-runner/src/handler.ts thin sweep handler isolate per-row failures
mocks: SES via injected SesEmailClient CountingSes through real dispatchOutbox
SQS via aws-sdk-client-mock / injected sender ({outbox_id} only)
EventBridge NOT tested (test sweep query + advance directly)
plan.notes: Schema exists (0005): campaigns + campaign_enrollments UNIQUE(campaign_id,profile_id) + (status,next_run_at) index — no migration. DSL: definition={startNode, nodes:{id:Node}}; Node=trigger{kind:segment_entry|event|manual,next}|wait{delay{seconds}|ISO8601|until,next}|condition{ast:§8,onTrue,onFalse}|action{kind:send|set_attribute,template_id?/key?/value?,next}|exit. Re-enrollment 'once' default (structural ON CONFLICT (campaign_id,profile_id) DO NOTHING + decideReenrollment); reenroll_after_exit specified but off (UNIQUE has no status component; true re-entry needs versioned key, documented). Idempotent advance = CAS on updated_at (UPDATE WHERE updated_at=$expected RETURNING) so concurrent sweeps/retries advance at most once; action sends also rely on outbox dedupe_key + dispatcher atomic claim for exactly-once SEND. Runner re-implements NONE of suppression/cap/quiet — all campaign sends via real dispatchOutbox. Sweep cross-workspace (each row carries workspace_id loaded from row never assumed); every WRITE workspace-scoped via runStatementsInWorkspaceTx. Mock SES+SQS at boundary; real PG for integration gated hasDatabaseUrl/skipIf. Don't unit-test scheduler fired. Reuse runStatementsInWorkspaceTx (dispatcher/broadcast) not runPlanInWorkspaceTx for these writes.

assertions: validateCampaignDefinition: one trigger, unique ids, resolvable edges, reachable exit
rejects no/2+ trigger, dangling edge, dup id
rejects unknown node/trigger types
wait requires exactly one of delay|until
condition requires §8 AST + onTrue + onFalse
action send requires template_id; set_attribute requires key+value
resolveStartNode/findNode
trigger -> advance to next immediately
wait{delay} -> stay nextRunAt=now+delay
wait{until future} -> nextRunAt=until stay
wait{until past} -> advance
condition matchesNow true->onTrue false->onFalse process immediately
action send -> advance + sideEffect enqueue_send
exit -> complete status=completed
pure
computeWaitNextRunAt {seconds} and ISO-8601 duration
until returns absolute date
isWaitElapsed boundary-inclusive
throws if no delay/until
buildBranchMatchQuery delegates to @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch, workspace_id values[0], AND p.id=$
unknown field/operator throws (whitelist)
non-empty match->onTrue empty->onFalse
null AST -> match-all -> onTrue
buildEnrollmentClaim CAS WHERE workspace_id=$1 AND id=$2 AND status=active AND updated_at=$3 RETURNING
buildAdvanceEnrollment sets current_node/next_run_at/status/updated_at guarded on prior updated_at, workspace_id $1
throws falsy workspaceId
buildSweepQuery status=active AND next_run_at<=now cross-workspace
RETURNING distinguishes winner/loser
buildEnrollmentInsert ON CONFLICT (campaign_id,profile_id) DO NOTHING current_node=start status=active workspace_id $1
decideReenrollment 'once' blocks while any prior exists
parseEnrollmentTrigger maps entered+segment_id to enrollment intent for campaign with matching trigger_segment_id; exited -> none
dedupe (campaign_id,profile_id)
buildCampaignDedupeKey(campaignId,profileId,nodeId) stable unique
buildCampaignOutboxInsert ON CONFLICT (dedupe_key) DO NOTHING campaign_id set workspace_id $1
buildDispatchEnqueueMessage {outbox_id} only no workspace_id
enqueue-only: no suppression/cap/quiet re-implemented
trigger->wait in one tick: claim, process trigger, land wait, next_run_at=now+delay, SQS not called
later tick wait->condition->action: evaluate branch, enqueue one send, advance to exit
exit -> status=completed clear next_run_at not re-swept
condition->immediate chains multiple non-wait nodes single tick
loop-guard MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK
sweep then runEnrollment per row; lost CAS claim skipped no-op
throwing enrollment isolated, others advance
thin wrapper, logic in injected core
real PG: campaign trigger->wait->condition->action(send)->exit; enroll; tick1 lands wait next_run_at=now+delay
advance clock + sweep: condition evals real profile_features via §8 compiler, routes action, one outbox row campaign_id status=pending
real dispatchOutbox (SES mocked): SES once with config_set, messages_log with campaign_id, outbox sent
final tick exit -> status=completed next_run_at NULL not re-swept
sweep doesn't return enrollment while now<next_run_at
no advance/outbox while wait pending
once now>=next_run_at sweep returns + advances; deferral by real sweep query/index
purchase_30d>=3: profile w/5 -> onTrue, w/1 -> onFalse vs real profile_features via buildSegmentMatch
branch scoped workspace_id $1 + AND p.id=$profile
onTrue/onFalse target written to current_node
two concurrent runEnrollment on same due enrollment: CAS on updated_at -> exactly one wins advances, loser no-op
advances exactly one step no double-advance
due action under concurrent sweep -> one outbox row (ON CONFLICT dedupe_key)
replay same tick after complete -> no-op (stale updated_at matches no row)
segment_change_log entered+segment_id=S -> one enrollment at start node workspace-scoped
second entered same (campaign,P) -> no row (ON CONFLICT)
exited -> no enrollment
other workspace's segment never enrolls here
campaign status!=active -> no enroll
action send -> outbox -> real dispatchOutbox passes gate->suppression->cap->quiet before SES
suppressed recipient -> campaign send SKIPPED SES not called
workspace not active/verified -> refused at gate
clean send -> messages_log campaign_id + usage emails_sent +1
runner never calls SES
every write binds workspace_id $1 (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx asserts values[0])
two workspaces same campaign shape + same external_id advance independently no cross-bleed
cross-workspace sweep claims each row under its own workspace_id from the row
cross-phase: segment entry->enrollment->sweep->wait->branch(§8)->action->real dispatchOutbox->messages_log driving actual cores
happy path: status=completed, one messages_log campaign_id, outbox sent, workspace config_set on send
isolation peer workspace unaffected

instructions: Clear the turbo cache and RUN the tests yourself (DATABASE_URL set) twice from cold, plus pnpm typecheck and pnpm lint. For UI phases, RUN the Playwright e2e in a real browser. Record real pass/fail. Confirm the integration tier RUNS (not skipped) and no regression in earlier phases.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is covered by a passing, non-vacuous test: ["An enrolled profile advances through trigger -> wait -> condition -> action -> exit.","A wait defers until next_run_at; a branch (condition) routes correctly using the §8 compiler against profile/features/membership.","The runner is idempotent (no double-advance on retry / concurrent sweep).","Campaign action sends pass through the Dispatcher guards (suppression/cap/quiet-hours); enrollment via segment entry works; re-enrollment policy is enforced. Everything workspace-scoped."].

Highest-priority checks: (a) tenant isolation + role enforcement (server-side, not just UI-hidden); (b) all sends route through the Dispatcher guards; (c) idempotency (broadcast dedupe / no double-advance / idempotent rollups); (d) AWS mocked/LocalStack (no real AWS) yet asserted; (e) for UI, the flow actually works in a real browser against the real backend, not a stub.
If any integration test mocks Postgres, that is a FAIL. If a UI criterion is only unit-tested (no real browser), that is a FAIL.
Scope check: flag any feature beyond the cited sections; verify the §20 cost model is exact.
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, browserVerified: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity, tenant isolation, roles, and (for UI) real-browser behavior
prompt.task: Independently verify "Campaign workflow engine" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself (cold cache); do not trust the self-report.
